content.text: Having multiple sources of income can be
a blessing, and that's something I
really struggled with when I started my
freelance career. I already made a video
about how much I made across multiple
income streams last year. But in this
video, we're going to talk about eight
different ways you can make passive
income. And for each one, I'm going to
tell you three things. How hard it is to
get started, how long it takes before
you see your first rupee, and how
passive it actually is once you have
built the thing. Now obviously
everything that you see in this video
took years to build. So don't expect to
be hitting those number when you're just
starting. But this generally is the
video which I wish I had when I was
starting when I was trying to build
passive income. When I say passive
income, I want to be very careful
because there is truly nothing like
passive income. There's no way to make
money without doing nothing at all. What
passive income actually means is making
money which is not directly tied to your
time. For example, I spent a few months
building an online course last year and
now that course sells while I'm
sleeping, while I'm on vacation or while
I'm recording this video. I did the work
once and now the course keep earning.
That's what passive income actually
means. The other thing I want to say up
front, all these things take time. There
is literally no get rich quick scheme.
Everything that you see in this video
will take days to weeks to even years.
And the only way to make passive income
is to provide value in a way that is not
directly tied to your time. So for every
income stream, I'm going to give it
three ratings. First is getting started.
This is how easy or hard it is to
actually begin. And the scale goes from
very easy, meaning anyone can start
today with zero experience and zero
money all the way to very hard meaning
it requires serious skill, capital or
technical knowledge to even get off the
ground. Second is time to first rupee.
This is how long it realistically takes
before you see actual money. It ranges
from days, meaning you could earn almost
immediately all the way to 1 year plus,
meaning you need to be in for the long
game before anything meaningful comes
in. And third is passive score. This is
how hands off it actually is once you
have built the thing. From not passive,
meaning you still have to show up and
grind regularly, all the way to fully
passive, meaning once it's set up, it
earns with almost no ongoing effort from
you. We will cover all eight passive
income methods. Method five and eight
are the ones I personally rate the
highest. So, make sure you stick around
for those. That being said, method one
is content creation or YouTube. I am
obviously going to talk about it because
YouTube is a big part of how I make
money. So, how easy it is to get started
on YouTube? Just getting started is very
easy. You go to YouTube, create a
channel, film something on your phone,
and upload it. That's genuinely it.
Anyone can start today. But here's the
thing. Getting started is the easy part.
Getting any attention or views is the
difficult part. Because to grow on
YouTube, you need to understand multiple
things at once. How the algorithm works,
how to catch someone's attention in the
first few seconds. And a basic
understanding of human psychology and
why people click and watch. So, while
starting is very easy, getting good at
these things will take months to years.
Time to first rupee is close to 1 year
plus. To even qualify for monetization,
you need 1,000 subscribers and 4,000
watch hours. I started my YouTube
channel in August 2023, and it got
monetized by December 2023. But I did
not see any meaningful income for the
next 1.5 years. All I was making was $
20 to $30 per month. That's over a year
of uploading consistently before the
money started making sense. So, if
you're thinking you'll start this month
and you'll start earning by next few
months, that's mostly not going to
happen. Passive score. I would say it's
very passive. Here's what makes YouTube
genuinely powerful as a long-term income
stream. Older videos keep earning. In
2025, my YouTube AdSense generated 5
lakh 76,000 rupees and a big chunk of
that came from older videos. These days,
I get close to 50,000 rupees every month
from YouTube ads just from the past
videos. Now, it's not fully passive
because you have to keep uploading and
keep showing up. But the older content
working in the background is genuinely
passive. And on top of AdSense, a
growing YouTube channels opens up
multiple income streams like brand
deals, affiliate marketing courses, etc.
Now, how do you get started? I have
already made a complete video on how to
start content creation and start
monetizing. I'll add the link in the

a digital product. Now, this is one of

ebook, a notion template, a swipe file,
literally anything that solves a
specific problem for a specific person.
Let me give you some real examples of
people doing this. The first one is Da
and she is selling a business book club

He took his knowledge, wrote it down and

three completely different industries,

and sell it forever. Now, how difficult

You can write an ebook in Google Docs

starting part itself very doable. Next
time to first rupee, I would say weeks

list. If you already have an audience,

this is relatively quicker. Next passive
score I would say it's fully passive.
This is as passive as it gets. You build

problem you have already solved for

And the thing is affiliate marketing is

Getting started is easy, but actually
getting someone to buy from your link is
the hard part. So getting results
without an audience is very difficult.
That's why I put YouTube or content

rupee, I would say it's few weeks to

I would say this is fully passive. This

A link you place in your video

number four is creating an online

everything else. But here's what
happened after I launched the course in

and after that it settled into
consistent passive income stream and it

lifetime earning of this course is close

Then you need to structure the content

That's exactly what happened with me.
The launch month was the biggest but

That's what passive income actually

Pick one skill you have that people are

rated bond access to retail investor.
Here is what that means in very simple

for a fixed tenure and in return you

These bonds are generally rated between

before maturity if needed. What I

The investing experience is seamless
with UPI and net banking integrations.
Vintealth even invests 2% in all the

It's just another way to diversify your

reality for most people in India.
Average person maybe has 5,000 10,000

10,000 rupees a month compounding at

You can just open account in 10 minutes

100 rupees. Now the barrier to entry has

The longer your money stays invested,
